---
title: "Diagrams as code — Mermaid & Graphviz"
description: "Clinical decision algorithms written as plain text and compiled to crisp vector SVG. Version-control your flowcharts."
image: thumbnail.svg
image-alt: "Diagrams as code — Mermaid & Graphviz — Blood Doctor · Quarto"
date: "2026-07-17"
categories: [Visualisation]
engine: markdown
---

Drawing a flowchart by hand in PowerPoint is slow, and the moment guidance
changes you redraw it. Quarto lets you *write* diagrams as text and compiles them
to scalable vector graphics.

## A decision algorithm in plain text

````markdown
```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  A["Chromatogram"] --> B{"HbS present?"}
  B -->|Yes| C["Evaluate % HbS"]
  B -->|No|  D["Evaluate % HbA2"]
  C -->|38–45%| E["Sickle cell trait"]
  D -->|3.5–7%| G["β-thalassaemia trait"]
```
````

That text renders as a labelled, colour-coded flowchart. Because it is text, it
**diffs** cleanly in git — you can see exactly how a pathway changed between
versions, and reviewers can comment on a line.

**Mermaid** covers flowcharts, sequence diagrams, Gantt charts and mind-maps;
**Graphviz** (`dot`) handles dense graphs and inheritance trees. Both are built in.

::: {.callout-tip}
## See the gallery
The [**Diagrams & algorithms page**](/diagrams/algorithms.qmd) compiles several
clinical pathways, including the HbS / HbA2 interpretation tree.
:::
